title: Andrés Engler -Services Hiring Manager Interview | Technical Managing Editor
meeting_title: Andrés Engler -Services Hiring Manager Interview | Technical Managing Editor

transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Nice to meet you.
Thank you for your time.
Yeah, thanks for scheduling some time with me.
Are you in Argentina?
Amazing, want to say it's Argentina.
Amazing.
Very fun.
So, winter there.
were just talking to somebody else.
She's not in Argentina.
I forget where she is.
But she was like, oh, it's so cold today.
And everybody else on the call in the U.S.
was like, oh, my God, I want to be cold.
I miss being cold right now.
Where are you based right now?
I'm in Rhode Island, which is like two hours north of New York.
Okay, great.
Yeah, I haven't been there, but I know New York, and I've heard about that city.
Yeah, right now, it's not really cold, but this winter was very hard.
Oh, I bet.
Yeah, we are surprised by that.
that.
Um, well, great to meet you.
Thank you so much again for scheduling time.
Really excited to, uh, to chat with you.
Um, I can start off by giving you a bit of background on myself and kind of how I'm thinking about the role and how it fits within growthx.
Um, this is like a huge priority for us.
So like, we're really excited to be talking to people.
Um, and we're hiring, um, there are about, I think there are 12 managing editors at growthx right now.
We're hiring a couple of people into this role.
Um, so what a managing editor does within the ecosystem of growthx, um, is it's sort of like a project management role with two, um, really two focuses.
Um, part of that project management is, um, tracking kind of client side deliverables and thinking about, um, um,
You know, we deliver content generated by AI for our clients.

So some of that is, you know, like making sure that the client is updated on status of projects, making sure that they actually like you're responsible for making sure that things are published and delivered on time.
And so through that, there's an editorial aspect.
So project management in terms of editorial and communication with client.
And then the other piece of that is sort of on the technical side.
We use our own proprietary platform that runs AI workflows to generate the content.
You'll work in a pod.
So there will be a content manager who actually runs the workflows in the platform.
And then we'll hand them over to you to edit.
And then on the other side, you'll work with a director who kind of helps manage the strategic direction, roadmap, long-term vision with the client of like the goals of the engagement.
with work work work with a a Thank
But, you know, all of this is built on our proprietary platform where we're running these workflows and generating the content.
And kind of as the project manager, you'll be helping think about where the platform is falling short, where it's working, where it's not working, and how we make it better.

And trying to develop kind of technical feedback that the engineering team can action on with your, like, editorial mindset, but also, like, understanding kind of how AI works, AI prompting, and how it might make the workflow more effective and efficient, so that you can do, like, less editing and more, like, overall project management and kind of client strategic work.
Mm-hmm.
So that's a high level about the role.
I manage customer operations at GrowthX, so kind of where I'm sitting within that is I'm looking at the overall process, the makeup of our...

Odds and kind of capacity for client engagement, and so thinking about how the system works as a whole, where our processes are and aren't working, and sort of managing that from like a background position.
So that's kind of how I fit into this as well.
Do have any questions?
Not really.
Maybe we can talk a little bit about what type of answers should I give to the engineering team?
Usually, what type of, I don't know, responses do they expect from me in the future?
Yeah.
So it's mostly like filing bug reports and things like that.
Okay, great.
When the workflow runs, we have specific inputs that we're giving.
that's why air prompting is incredibly important in this role.
So you're generating the artifacts and prompts that.

Go into generating the workflows, it's feedback about like, hey, like we expected this kind of outcome, we didn't get that, like help us understand why, and then the engineering team will run into it.
So it's sort of like bug reporting, but a level higher and a little bit more detailed, because you should have an understanding of like AI prompting and the outcomes you're expecting.
Okay, great.
Yes, right now I'm, I'm helping them to make their content like, with their international expansion.
And right now I'm reading all the content and saying, hey, we are, this is not working, we need to fix this.
And I talked to the engineering team like all day, trying to make that work.
So yeah, awesome.
Perfect.
Yeah.
Well, let's jump into your experience.
Specifically, why don't you give me like kind of a high level overview of your career to date?
And really, I'd love to know what you're looking for next, too.
Yeah, for sure.
I've started working as a journalist in Argentina at the age of 19, working at the leading financial newspaper in Spanish.
After five years, I've started working with U.S.-based companies as a reporter first, and then as an editor.
I was the Latin American editor at Coindes.
And then I started working at the product side, the strategy.
Right now, I'm helping with all the partnerships, AI-related things.
So I started as a journalist, and I transitioned to being an editor right now.
I just like to create good products, and that includes working with AI.
So right now, I'm working with all the international strategy at Coindes.
via my own studio, I have an NNC company based in the U.S., I provide services.
So different startups, investment funds, non-profits, but my main client right now is Coindesk.
And I'm just working with the engineering team, the product team, to release different languages.
Right now we have more than A, but we plan to launch between 10 and 20 in the next few months.
So yeah, that's my expertise right now.
I'm working with product strategy and AI.
At the same time.
So I don't know if I'm a journalist right now, but I love the, yeah, that job.
I love design, I love products, I love AI.
So I don't love writing right now.
I prefer other things.
But yeah, I'm good at noticing errors, trying to fix them as soon as possible.
And yeah, I love creating good stuff, to be honest.
Yeah.
So...
Talk to me a little bit more about your work with Coindesk, and you said you're working with the product engineering and AI.
Can you give me specifically examples of how you're working with each of those teams?
Yeah, for sure.
We released two months ago.
We were using Depot for translations, and I've advised the companies to start working with OpenAI.
So right now, we are trying to translate it with ChatGPT.
And it was very hard because we had to integrate ChatGPT with Sanity, which is right now our CMS.
So that required a lot of time with the engineering team.
I was helping them to refine our interface because different languages need different interfaces.
That's harder with Chinese and Korean, for example.
And right now, I'm just reviewing all the content in all the languages.
We are refining UI.
And all the interfaces.
And specifically with Spanish, I'm trying to edit all the articles that we are publishing.
And at the same time, we are trying to hire different people in different parts of the world who speak different languages.
So that's very hard.
So, yeah, that's my role right now.
I don't know if that answered your question.
I can go into more details if necessary.
Yeah, talk to me about that integration into ChatGPT.
I'm guessing you guys are using an API to build it into Sanity.
Yes, we are using an API.
But right now, we are trying to fix the prompting because we are sending guidelines, but they are not working.
So the engineering team is working on that.
I don't have, like, all the expertise necessary to fix that.
And I'm trying to rely on the engineering team.
As you may know, like, legacy media is, like, yeah.
A lot, a lot of teams.
So it takes a lot of time to get things fixed.
So that's why I love to work with a startup right now, because all the things that need to be changed take, takes like a lot, a lot of months.
And so, yeah, it's very hard.
Yeah.
Talk to me about fixing the prompting.
Like, what do you think is wrong with it?
What's not working?
And I mean, I don't mean from a technical perspective, but like, how are you playing with that piece?
Yes, that's a good question.
I was preparing prompting using like JSON.
And we were using Sanity for that.
And we were like, trying to create two promptings, different promptings.
The first one was for meta, and the other one was for headlines, subheads, and concepts, like the main paragraphs.
I don't know why it's not working.
I started playing with ChatGPT.
We're trying to create the right prompting, and I'm just waiting for the engineering team to tell me what's going on.
But we have a special section on Sanity, focused on AI, so we are sending all the prompts to that section.
I was playing with the staging platform, but it is not working, to be honest.
Well, how do you think about prompting, in general, when you're working with AI?
Wow.
Sorry, I didn't start the question.
I love, like, prompting, learning.
I've been doing this for, yeah, one year, and I feel that I need to learn a lot, because every day is, like, crazy.
I mean, from, like, I don't know how to explain this, but I need to change my brain in order to...
Yeah, like make the right questions.
And so I'm learning to do that to change the way I'm thinking.
Tell me more about that.
What do you mean?
What I mean?
I feel that I have been working as a journalist for more than 10 years, and I don't know how to ask the right questions.
And at the age of 33, I'm learning to ask the right questions.
And the way you, because sometimes I have all these platforms available, and I'm not like conscious that they can fix things.
And it requires a lot of time to learn that and to ask the right questions and to say, I need help with this, we'll deal with this, and be very detailed at the same time.
It takes a lot of time.
So yeah, I'm just trying to learn that.
Yeah.
Um...
Well, so for Coinbase, have you ever worked with external-facing clients, or have you managed client relationships?
I've been working with startups, some nonprofits, and so I have experience working with CMOs, CEOs.
Right now at Coinbase, I'm not giving explanations or answers to my boss.
He's the head of SEO, but I have experience working with this CEO of Coinbase as well.
So I have experience.
It is not my day by day, right?
I'm working within a team right now at the company, but I have experience working with several founders at the same time when I was providing services via my studio.
So yeah, and no, right now I'm focused on all things, but I have experience doing that.
Yeah.
How do you think about...
for I get a car.
So prioritizing your time, mean, even at Coindesk, when you're working with a bunch of different teams, you know, how do you prioritize?
Yeah, right now I have to edit between 15 and 20 articles, Spanish language articles per day.
So I try to do that during my mornings, like four or five hours of deep work.
Then I move to, I try to move all my meetings to my afternoons.
So after the deep work, I just have meetings.
And then after those meetings, I try to create a strategy for the next day.
I use Notion as my second brain.
I'm totally dependent on it.
So yeah, that's my, that's, yeah, I think my workflow right now.
Okay.
And can you tell me about a time when you came into a process that was maybe not optimal or not the way that you would have done it and changed it or improved it?
Oh, yeah.
That was the case of the translations that Coindesk was, was.
They were using Depot and Google Translate, and the quality was terrible.
So it took between one month and two months to decide after several trials which platform we should use.
And I decided to start with OpenAI.
And the quality improved a lot, so we improved our traffic three times in just one month.
So that was a crazy result.
And that was because of a decision I made.
And it was quite easy to change that.
I mean, it took several weeks, but for a traditional media company, was good.
Yeah, how did you get buy-in to make that decision?
Sorry?
How did you get everybody else to agree to that decision?
Change had to be made.
Yeah.
The answer is easy.
I think traffic was not doing well and they couldn't find the reasons.
And I came to the conclusion that the reason was the quality and people were not engaging with the content because of the quality.
And that was because Google Translate and Depot were not as good as ChatGPT.
And that was a time when ChatGPT like improved a lot, the translations.
So it was a great moment to transition to that system.
So when we started with ChatGPT, we waited like one week and the results were incredible.
And it was very easy to, after the staging, to like release that finally.
Yeah.
Okay, great.
And then, yeah, talk to me about like on
As you're going through this like translations project, how are you thinking about continually improving the feedback loop with engineering?
Because it seems like you are working with them a lot.
So talk to about how that started and how that relationship has evolved.
Yeah, for sure.
We have meetings every week, Mondays, with the engineering team and the product team as well.
So I create a list on different tickets on JIRA.
So I'm starting asking all the things that need to be fixed.
And we check them like every week, every Monday.
To be honest, it is taking enough time to get things fixed because of the times that the company is managing and right now is working on different projects.
And this engineering team is quite small considering all the things that need to be.

So I just create like tickets in JIRA explaining all the things that need to be fixed, I always try to propose solutions, I start playing on ChatGPT if I don't know, like all the answers or CloudE or Gemini, Gemini, we call Gemini here in Argentina, sorry, and yeah, if I don't know something, I just start exploring the potential.
solutions in order to gain time, knowing that they are dealing with a lot of things and I'm not a priority.
So I always do that.
I create a ticket and I say, hey, I think we should try this because of this, of this, of this, and yeah, I just send them.
Yeah.
At the same time, we, we, we usually send like proofreaders, different articles to check.
And we are trying to create a system to review that content every X amount of time.
Yeah.
All right.
Well, those are all the questions I have.
What questions do you have for me?
I think the process was really clear.
I have all the details.
I know that we are going to work life, if I am selected, like four or five hours during the afternoon of ET, right?
Because we need to work with a team based in San Francisco.
Is that right?
Mm-hmm.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
Great.
And no, yeah, I think we are good.
Maybe if you could give me like more details about...
About the quantity of clients, how it'd be like managing?
Yeah.
So the managing editor, the goal is for this person to manage like three to five clients on an ongoing basis.
And so that means having a weekly call with those clients, talking to them on Slack, we have Slack channels with all of our clients, and delivering like eight to 10 pieces of content per client.
So you'd be editing probably 30, 50 pieces of content a week.
Okay.
Okay.
That's good.
And what type of stakeholder I'm going to be talking to, like CMOs, content managers?
It's company.
Okay.
But we do have, usually have very high level buy-in.
Yeah.
So talking to C-level is not unusual for these roles.
Okay.
That's good, yes.
Okay.
All right.
Is there anything else I can answer for you?
I think we are good for now.
Okay.
Andrés, was great to meet you.
Thank you so much for your time.
And stay warm, I guess.
Yeah, for sure.
I would try.
All right.
Have a good one.
Okay.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Interview with Andrés Engler for Services Hiring Manager (Technical Managing Editor) role at GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Role involves project management, client communication, and technical feedback on AI content generation platform](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Andrés has extensive experience in journalism, editing, and AI-driven content strategy, particularly with Coindesk](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [GrowthX is hiring multiple managing editors to handle 3-5 clients each, delivering 8-10 content pieces per client weekly](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
  - [Position requires balancing editorial work, client management, and technical understanding of AI prompting](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0)

## Topics

### Role Overview

  - [Project management focus: client-side deliverables and AI-generated content](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Editorial responsibilities: ensuring timely publication and delivery](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=136.0)
  - [Technical aspect: working with proprietary AI platform, providing feedback for improvements](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [Team structure: work in a pod with content manager and director](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=157.0)
  - [Client management: weekly calls, Slack communication, potential C-level interactions](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)

### Candidate Background

  - [Started as a journalist in Argentina at 19, worked for leading financial newspaper](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Transitioned to U.S.-based companies as reporter, then editor](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [Currently working on product strategy and AI integration at Coindesk](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [Manages international expansion, content review, and AI implementation](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
  - [Operates own studio providing services to startups, investment funds, and non-profits](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=412.0)

### AI Experience

  - [Advised Coindesk to switch from DeepL to OpenAI for translations](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [Worked on integrating ChatGPT with Sanity CMS](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)
  - [Involved in refining UI for different languages and reviewing multi-language content](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=527.0)
  - [Experience with AI prompting, including JSON formatting and troubleshooting](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=632.0)

### Work Style and Skills

  - [Prioritizes deep work in mornings (4-5 hours), meetings in afternoons](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
  - [Uses Notion as a "second brain" for organization](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
  - [Comfortable working with engineering teams and filing detailed bug reports](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
  - [Demonstrates ability to drive significant improvements (e.g., 3x traffic increase at Coindesk)](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=934.0)
  - [Eager to learn and adapt, especially in AI prompting and asking the right questions](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)

### GrowthX Specifics

  - [12 current managing editors, hiring multiple for this role](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
  - [Managing editors expected to handle 3-5 clients, 30-50 content pieces weekly](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=1232.0)
  - [Work hours align with ET afternoon (4-5 hours) to collaborate with San Francisco team](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=1184.0)
  - [High-level stakeholder communication, including potential C-suite interactions](https://fathom.video/share/7fa4wjGwpXfSQJ4YLj_NyRwbgYVFNZ5J?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)

## Next Steps

  - Evaluate Andrés' fit for the role based on his experience and skills
  - Consider his adaptability and eagerness to learn, particularly in AI prompting
  - Assess his ability to manage multiple clients and high-volume content editing
  - Determine if his background in journalism and transition to AI-driven content aligns with GrowthX's needs
